title: On Snow
content:
From Heaven I fall, though from earth I begin,
    No lady alive can show such a skin.
    I'm bright as an angel, and light as a feather,
    But heavy and dark, when you squeeze me together.
    Though candour and truth in my aspect I bear,
    Yet many poor creatures I help to ensnare.
    Though so much of Heaven appears in my make,
    The foulest impressions I easily take.
    My parent and I produce one another,
    The mother the daughter, the daughter the mother.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Jonathan Swift delivers a powerful performance in "On Snow"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
